text: {"title":"Activity Modality Balance","group_name":"activity_modality_balance","family":"activity_composition","summary":"Capture whether a student's movement pattern is primarily structured exercise, incidental daily ambulation, broadly active, or behaviorally inconsistent with their stated activity level.","depends_on":[],"strategy":"Use step_count, exercise_duration, calorie_expenditure, and physical_activity_level only. Fit train-only unsupervised reference points: medians, IQRs, and tertile cut points for the numeric activity fields. For each row, impute numeric inputs for calculations with train medians and use a small positive denominator guard. Create robust quantile scores for steps, exercise minutes, and calorie expenditure; derive activity_volume as the mean of step and exercise quantiles, modality_delta as step quantile minus exercise quantile, structured_share as exercise_duration divided by exercise_duration plus step_count/1000 plus 1, ambulatory_share as step_count/1000 divided by the same denominator, calorie_intensity_residual as calorie quantile minus activity_volume, and self_report_gap as ordinal physical_activity_level minus activity_volume where sedentary=0, moderate=0.5, active=1, and missing=0.5. Add one compact modality bucket from step/exercise tertiles: low_both, walk_dominant, exercise_dominant, high_both, or mixed_mid. Background used: https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2025/25_0118.htm, https://www.cdc.gov/healthyschools/professional_development/e-learning/cspap/page04.html, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s44167-024-00045-9.","expected_signal":"Balanced accuracy may improve because fit students may show coherent high-both or well-aligned active patterns, unhealthy students may concentrate in low-both or overreported-activity patterns, and at-risk students may appear as one-sided or inconsistent movement styles that raw steps and exercise minutes do not separate cleanly.","risk":"This may be redundant with existing activity-related raw features and prior density or strain groups; unsupervised quantile cut points can shift if train and test activity distributions differ, and the modality bucket is coarse enough to lose signal near tertile boundaries."}
